intro: Lasiochloa capitata, also known as the "false pampas grass," is a type of flowering plant native to South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving respiratory function due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Lasiochloa capitata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
